title: Expose full src edit toolset and align proposal execution guidance
summary: Expose all existing src-edit tools in tool definitions and update proposal execution instructions so executors choose the correct src tool by operation (replace/insert/delete/create) instead of defaulting to replace-only.
details:
## Why this change
- Proposal execution currently biases toward replace-style edits even when insert/delete/create are better fits.
- Source evidence:
  - `src/gateway/tools/defs/file-web-memory.ts` lines ~218-256 define only `find_replace_source` and `replace_lines_source` for src write operations.
  - `src/gateway/agents-runtime/subagent-executor.ts` lines ~489-590 already implement additional src write tools: `insert_after_source`, `delete_lines_source`, and `write_source`.
  - `src/gateway/routes/proposals.router.ts` line ~163 currently instructs executors to apply src edits using `find_replace_source (preferred) or replace_lines_source`, creating a strong preference for replace-only workflows.

## Exact source edits
- `src/gateway/tools/defs/file-web-memory.ts` (around src-edit tool definitions, ~218 onward):
  - Current flow summary: exposes only two src write tools (`find_replace_source`, `replace_lines_source`) to model tool schemas.
  - Planned edit: add three additional src-edit tool definitions with full parameter schemas and proposal-session scope language:
    - `insert_after_source(file, after_line, content)`
    - `delete_lines_source(file, start_line, end_line)`
    - `write_source(file, content, overwrite?)`
  - Keep existing `find_replace_source` and `replace_lines_source` unchanged.
- `src/gateway/routes/proposals.router.ts` (execution prompt assembly, around lines ~130-165):
  - Current flow summary: src tool block lists insert/delete but not write; instruction step 2 still says “find_replace_source (preferred) or replace_lines_source”.
  - Planned edit:
    - Add `write_source(file, content, overwrite?)` to the SRC EDIT TOOLS list in `srcToolBlock`.
    - Replace step 2 wording with operation-based guidance, e.g. use the most appropriate src tool:
      - `find_replace_source` for exact text swaps
      - `replace_lines_source` for line-range replacement
      - `insert_after_source` for insertion
      - `delete_lines_source` for deletions
      - `write_source` for new src files (or explicit overwrite)
    - Remove “preferred” language that over-biases tool choice.
- `src/gateway/teams/team-dispatch-runtime.ts` (tool blocklist section around lines ~140-144):
  - Current flow summary: when agents cannot propose, blocklist includes `find_replace_source` and `replace_lines_source` only.
  - Planned edit: extend blocklist parity to include `insert_after_source`, `delete_lines_source`, and `write_source` so src-edit gating stays consistent across all src write tools.
- `src/gateway/tasks/background-task-runner.ts` (MUTATE_TOOLS near lines ~48-54):
  - Current flow summary: mutate detection includes only `find_replace_source` and `replace_lines_source` among src tools.
  - Planned edit: include `insert_after_source`, `delete_lines_source`, and `write_source` in `MUTATE_TOOLS` so stall-reset semantics are consistent when these tools are used.

## Deterministic behavior after patch
- Happy path:
  - Proposal execution sessions will expose all implemented src write primitives in the tool schema and in runtime guidance.
  - Executor can choose the correct tool for the edit shape (replace/insert/delete/create), reducing awkward replace-only patches.
- Edge cases:
  - Existing replace-based proposals continue to work unchanged.
  - `write_source` remains restricted to proposal/code_exec sessions and src-only paths as already enforced in executor runtime.
- Error handling:
  - Existing executor-side guardrails remain unchanged (session prefix checks, src path boundaries, overwrite checks).
- Explicit non-changes:
  - No change to authorization model for src edits.
  - No change to non-src file editing tools.

## Acceptance tests
1. Build/typecheck:
   - Run `npm run build` from repo root; expect success.
2. Tool schema verification:
   - In a session with src-edit permissions, verify available tools include:
     `find_replace_source`, `replace_lines_source`, `insert_after_source`, `delete_lines_source`, `write_source`.
3. Proposal prompt verification:
   - Approve a src proposal and inspect generated execution prompt:
     - SRC EDIT TOOLS list includes `write_source`.
     - step 2 uses operation-based guidance rather than “find_replace_source (preferred)”.
4. Runtime behavior checks:
   - Execute one test proposal that uses each of `insert_after_source`, `delete_lines_source`, and `write_source`; confirm edits apply and task does not false-stall.
5. Team dispatch gating check:
   - For a non-proposing team agent, confirm these three additional src tools are blocked consistently with existing src write tools.

## Risks and compatibility
- Potential risks:
  - Slight increase in tool-choice surface area could lead to inconsistent style unless prompt guidance is clear.
- Backward compatibility:
  - Fully backward compatible; existing flows using replace tools continue unchanged.
- Mitigations/rollback:
  - Keep operation-based instruction concise and deterministic.
  - If regressions occur, revert only prompt wording while leaving schema parity improvements intact.

affectedFiles.description: Add insert_after_source, delete_lines_source, and write_source tool definitions for src edit sessions
Update src tool list and step-2 instructions to operation-based src tool selection; include write_source
Extend non-proposer tool blocklist to include all src write tools for consistent gating
Add missing src write tools to MUTATE_TOOLS so stall reset recognizes all src mutations

diffPreview:
- proposals.router step 2:
-   Apply edits using find_replace_source (preferred) or replace_lines_source.
+ proposals.router step 2:
+   Apply src edits with the tool that matches the operation:
+   - find_replace_source for exact text swaps
+   - replace_lines_source for line-range replacement
+   - insert_after_source for insertion
+   - delete_lines_source for deletion
+   - write_source for new src files (or overwrite when explicit)

+ file-web-memory.ts: add tool defs for insert_after_source, delete_lines_source, write_source under src-edit section
+ team-dispatch-runtime.ts: add insert_after_source/delete_lines_source/write_source to TEAM_BLOCKED_TOOLS when !canPropose
+ background-task-runner.ts: add insert_after_source/delete_lines_source/write_source to MUTATE_TOOLS
estimatedImpact: Aligns exposed src tooling with existing executor capabilities; reduces replace-only patching bias; improves deterministic execution guidance and stall detection consistency.
executorPrompt:
Execute approved src-edit proposal exactly as specified.

Requirements:
1) Re-read each affected file before editing to confirm current state and line contexts.
2) Apply only the listed edits to these files:
   - src/gateway/tools/defs/file-web-memory.ts
   - src/gateway/routes/proposals.router.ts
   - src/gateway/teams/team-dispatch-runtime.ts
   - src/gateway/tasks/background-task-runner.ts
3) For src paths, use src-edit tools only.
4) Do not make opportunistic refactors or unrelated changes.
5) Run npm run build once after edits. If build fails, stop and report exact failure.
6) Return a concise execution report with:
   - exact changed files
   - what changed per file
   - build result
   - any blockers/mismatches encountered.
